steps.title: Compute the upper bounds
steps.content:
The inequality is $y<6x+2$. For the x-values in the tables:
$$x=3 \Rightarrow 6x+2=20$$
$$x=5 \Rightarrow 6x+2=32$$
$$x=7 \Rightarrow 6x+2=44$$

steps.title: Check the table that looks promising
steps.content:
Choice C has y-values $16$, $28$, and $40$. Each is strictly below the matching upper bound:
$$16<20,\quad 28<32,\quad 40<44$$

steps.title: Eliminate the failed choices
steps.content: Choice A fails because it includes $y=20$ when $x=3$, and $20<20$ is false. Choice B fails because $36<32$ is false when $x=5$. Choice D fails because $24<20$ is false when $x=3$. So choice C is correct.
